title: NvmId::INVALID sentinel distinguishes auto-assign from overwrite; DataLost surfaces non-atomic delete-add hazard
description: nvm_write uses NvmId::INVALID (0) as a sentinel to distinguish 'create with server-assigned ID' from 'overwrite existing ID'. When overwriting, the delete-then-add is not atomic (NVM has no rollback), and if the add fails the original object is permanently lost. The WolfHsmError::DataLost { id } variant is returned in that case, carrying the affected ID so the caller has full diagnostic information. A future contributor might suggest treating a failed overwrite as a plain Ffi error, or trying to restore the old object. The NVM protocol has no rollback primitive; pretending the error is recoverable would be wrong. Naming it DataLost and carrying the ID is the correct honest contract: the caller knows exactly what was lost and can decide whether to write audit logs, alert, or halt.
